summary.intent: Dogfood the optimize-anything tool on real project artifacts (CLAUDE.md, README.md) to validate the LLM judge path works end-to-end, and design the next major feature (analyze subcommand / plateau-detection) based on findings.
summary.outcome: Completed dogfood runs on both CLAUDE.md and README.md, discovered three distinct bottlenecks in the LLM judge optimization loop, restructured the README (-118 lines, all doc contract tests passing), fixed a weight comment bug in skill_clarity.sh, created design docs for the analyze subcommand, and committed two clean commits. Key product insight captured: auto-refine is not the core product but a targeted feature for one specific failure mode.

repo: All four Tier-3 open items (CI workflow, --run-dir autosave, score history, spec-file loader) were already code-complete in commit 9dc9448 — the backlog was stale
skill_clarity.sh had a weight comment mismatch: header said structure=0.30/actionability=0.30/specificity=0.25/conciseness=0.15 but actual WEIGHTS dict was 0.25/0.25/0.20/0.30
docs/ is in .gitignore — design docs and handoff files are local-only reference files, not tracked by git
dogfood_runs/ needed to be added to .gitignore to avoid tracking optimization run artifacts
gepa's default proposer (reflection_lm) may be unavailable — must always pass --model explicitly

The doc contract test (tests/test_doc_contract.py) enforces that README.md mentions specific intake schema terms: artifact_class, hard_constraints, evaluator_cwd, best_artifact, total_metric_calls, score_summary, top_diagnostics, plateau_guidance, evaluator_failure_signal

code.finding: Header comment listed wrong weights; actual WEIGHTS dict (canonical) is structure=0.25, actionability=0.25, specificity=0.20, conciseness=0.30
Enforces README mentions specific output schema keys and intake schema terms — any README restructuring must preserve these terms

workflow: LLM judge with vague objectives compresses scoring range (0.88 ceiling); quality_dimensions break through by providing gradient — same README scored 0.9175 with dimensions vs 0.88 without
gpt-4o-mini as proposer makes sweeping rewrites that lose as much as they gain on well-written docs; claude-sonnet makes structurally bolder but still penalized edits
Three distinct bottlenecks in LLM judge path: (1) evaluation criteria precision, (2) proposer capability, (3) judge conservatism toward restructuring
Auto-refine/analyze is specifically for --judge-model + vague objectives on near-ceiling artifacts; it does not apply to heuristic evaluators or HTTP evaluators
When optimization produces 0 improvements, the tool correctly preserves the original — failure mode is graceful, not degrading
Claude Sonnet as judge on structured markdown produces highly stable scores (0.88 across 9+ evaluations) — LLM judge variance concern is low for this use case
Cherry-picking structural improvements from failed mutations (rather than accepting whole rewrites) is more effective than full automated optimization on polished docs

friction: doc contract tests broke after README restructuring because intake schema terms (artifact_class, hard_constraints, best_artifact, etc.) were inadvertently removed — required a targeted re-add
The Tier-3 open items backlog was stale, causing wasted scoping effort at session start
gepa ran only 7 iterations despite budget=15 — unclear why it stopped early; needs investigation
Judge conservatism: Sonnet's mutation was objectively better UX (quickstart-first, concise examples) but scored lower — judge rewards information completeness over arrangement
LLM judge scoring variance of ±0.02 makes it hard to confirm genuine improvements at the margin
HANDOFF.md was not updated to point to the latest handoff doc — caught by user, required manual fix
open_items: Implement analyze subcommand: new CLI command + analyze_for_dimensions() in llm_judge.py — pre-optimization tool to discover quality dimensions
Implement plateau detection advisory: post-run suggestions when 0 improvements found (recommend stronger proposer, suggest quality_dimensions, flag judge conservatism)
CI verification in GitHub Actions — code exists but no evidence of a green run; secrets may not be configured
Architecture diagram — requested in previous session, still not built
Public release prep (item 4 from user's priority list)
Push 2 local commits (e891cfd, d0a7279) to origin when ready
Investigate why gepa stops at 7 iterations with budget=15
Evaluate whether judge calibration can be improved to reward structural improvements (quickstart-first layouts) rather than penalizing them
